name: SailPoint Identity Security Cloud
abstract: Enterprise workforce identity governance platform from the USA. SailPoint Identity Security Cloud (ISC) exposes an OAuth 2.0 / OpenID Connect layer primarily for API access and external application SSO. Supports authorization code and client credentials grants; PKCE not yet supported. Hosted on AWS with US, EU, and UK regions. FedRAMP Moderate authorized, SOC 2 Type II and ISO 27001 certified.

featureList.description: Implicit grant is not documented in SailPoint ISC OAuth 2.0 implementation.

Clients exchange an authorization code for an access_token after user login on ISC. Authorization endpoint: https://[tenant].login.sailpoint.com/oauth/authorize. Token endpoint: https://[tenant].api.identitynow.com/oauth/token.
SailPoint confirmed that ISC's authorization implementation does not currently support PKCE. It is being evaluated as part of potential OAuth 2.1 adoption, with no published timeline.
Pushed Authorization Requests (PAR) are not documented in SailPoint ISC.
Rich Authorization Requests (RAR) are not documented in SailPoint ISC.
JWT Secured Authorization Requests (JAR) are not documented in SailPoint ISC.
Client credentials grant is supported for machine-to-machine / service-to-service authentication without user involvement (scripts, programs, system integrations).
Resource owner password credentials grant is not documented for SailPoint ISC.
Clients use this grant type to exchange a refresh token for a new access_token when the existing one has expired.
OAuth 2.0 Token Exchange (RFC 8693) is not documented in SailPoint ISC.
Device Authorization Grant (RFC 8628) is not documented in SailPoint ISC.
Client-Initiated Backchannel Authentication (CIBA) is not documented in SailPoint ISC.
JWT Bearer grant type (RFC 7523) is not documented in SailPoint ISC.
SAML 2.0 Bearer grant type (RFC 7522) is not documented in SailPoint ISC.
Authorization endpoint at https://[tenant].login.sailpoint.com/oauth/authorize.
Token endpoint at https://[tenant].api.identitynow.com/oauth/token.
Userinfo endpoint at https://[tenant].api.identitynow.com/oauth/userinfo. Returns id, uid, email, phone, workPhone, firstname, lastname, capabilities, displayName, name.
Token revocation endpoint (RFC 7009) is not explicitly documented in SailPoint ISC.
A /oauth/info endpoint exists on the ISC API gateway but it is unclear whether it implements standard RFC 7662 token introspection or is a proprietary info endpoint.
Device authorization endpoint is not documented in SailPoint ISC.

A /.well-known/openid-configuration discovery document is not referenced in SailPoint developer documentation. Third-party integrations (e.g., Auth.js) configure endpoints manually rather than via auto-discovery.
OAuth Authorization Server metadata endpoint (RFC 8414) is not documented in SailPoint ISC.
The prompt=none parameter is not documented in SailPoint ISC's authorization request.
The prompt=login parameter is not documented in SailPoint ISC's authorization request.
The prompt=consent parameter is not documented in SailPoint ISC's authorization request.
The prompt=select_account parameter is not documented in SailPoint ISC's authorization request.
The prompt=create parameter is not documented in SailPoint ISC's authorization request.
Public client authentication (no client secret) is not documented in SailPoint ISC.

HTTP Basic authentication with client_id and client_secret may be supported as a standard OAuth 2.0 pattern, but is not explicitly documented. Documentation shows form-body (client_secret_post) as the primary method.
Client credentials submitted as form-encoded body parameters (client_id and client_secret in x-www-form-urlencoded body) are the documented authentication method for ISC token requests.
client_secret_jwt token endpoint authentication is not documented in SailPoint ISC.
private_key_jwt token endpoint authentication is not documented in SailPoint ISC.
Mutual TLS client authentication (RFC 8705) is not documented in SailPoint ISC.
Dynamic Client Registration (RFC 7591) is not supported. OAuth clients must be registered manually through the SailPoint ISC admin console.
Dynamic Client Registration Management (RFC 7592) is not supported in SailPoint ISC.
Refresh token rotation behavior is not documented in SailPoint ISC's OAuth implementation.

RP-Initiated Logout (OpenID Connect RP-Initiated Logout 1.0) is not documented in SailPoint ISC. SAML logout URLs are documented for federated SSO scenarios, but OIDC end_session_endpoint is not mentioned.
Demonstrating Proof of Possession (DPoP / RFC 9449) is not documented in SailPoint ISC.
FAPI 1.0 Baseline profile is not documented in SailPoint ISC.
FAPI 1.0 Advanced profile is not documented in SailPoint ISC.
FAPI 2.0 Security Profile is not documented in SailPoint ISC.
FAPI 2.0 Message Signing is not documented in SailPoint ISC.
JWT Secured Authorization Response Mode (JARM) is not documented in SailPoint ISC.
